methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sunfire, a Series E climate and energy company founded by Nils Aldag, has raised $315M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader Climate & Energy peer universe comprising 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

A Stage-Progression method was applicable, using the $315M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E advancement to the next financing stage. This approach yields an implied valuation of $441M. A Peer-Median approach could not be reliably applied; while 21 same-sector peers exist in the Climate & Energy universe, insufficient narrowly-filtered comparable companies (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) met the threshold of three or more datapoints necessary to establish a meaningful median. Using all-sector peers would introduce material distortion.

The triangulated range supported by this analysis is $375M to $507M, with a median point of $441M. This range reflects reliance on a single applicable method, which represents a material limitation. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage comparables to validate the stage-progression result and limited visibility into peer transaction multiples at this capital-intensive frontier-tech stage. A refined valuation would benefit from additional same-stage or recent-round private data from the Climate & Energy subsector.
